scope.summary: Author tailor_material_assignments migration and implement per-panel material override: add PanelSpec.material_preset_id field, the tailor_material_assignments authority table, and the assignment-write path with guard_authority_write and TailorGarmentMaterialAssigned event
acceptance_criteria: A dated migration YYYY_MM_DD_tailor_material_assignments.sql MUST create the tailor_material_assignments table with assignment_id TEXT PK ('ASGN-{uuid_v7}'), garment_id FK tailor_garments, panel_id TEXT, preset_id FK tailor_material_presets, event_ledger_event_id FK, created_at, with a UNIQUE(garment_id, panel_id) constraint

PanelSpec in tailor-solver/src/spec.rs MUST include an optional material_preset_id: Option<String> field; when present it references a tailor_material_presets.preset_id and overrides GarmentSpec.fabric for that panel at SolverMesh build time
build_solver_mesh MUST apply per-panel material_preset_id override AFTER the garment-level FabricProperties decode, not before; the override wins per-field

Every INSERT to tailor_material_assignments MUST call guard_authority_write(AuthorityMode::Postgres) and emit TailorGarmentMaterialAssigned with aggregate 'tailor_material_assignment' and the assignment_id

The tailor_garment_panel_assign_material MCP tool (spec §<N>.<i>.7.2) MUST write to tailor_material_assignments, not to GarmentSpec directly, so the assignment is independently auditable via the EventLedger
The FABRIC_PRESET_VALID ValidationDescriptor MUST verify that every material_preset_id referenced in a PanelSpec resolves to an existing tailor_material_presets row in the same workspace

An integration test MUST assert: assign leather preset to one panel of a multi-panel garment, assign chiffon to another, run simulation, verify the two panels have measurably different per-particle compliance values in the solver output
scope.risk_if_missed: MD's per-panel material override is a core garment design feature — a jacket with a leather body panel and a silk lining requires independent panel materials. Without the tailor_material_assignments table and per-panel override path, every panel in a GarmentSpec inherits the same garment-level fabric, making multi-material garments impossible. The spec explicitly requires this table (§<N>.<i>.4.4, §<N>.<i>.7.2) and the tailor_garment_panel_assign_material MCP tool calls it; MT-051 is too coarse to substitute because it does not name this table or the PanelSpec field.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
